calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jul-02-2012 Dismissal - No Appearance

MISCELLANEOUS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-28-2012 Sanctions
MINI MINUTES: No appearance. Order to Show Cause to Self-represented Plaintiff for failure to comply with San Francisco Superior Court Early Settlement Program Policies and Procedures No. 4. (Administrative fee) is heard this day. Plaintiff is sanctioned $400.00 for failure to appear. The OSC is not re-set at this time. Proceedings not reported. (610)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Request For Admissions Admitted, Compel Responses To Discovery, And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-18-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

MARGARET A. BURTON, ESQ., FARBSTEIN & BLACKMAN, TEL. 650.554.6200, FOR DEFENDANT AND MOVING PARTY FAMILY SERVICE AGENCY OF SAN FRANCISCO.

NO APPEARANCE BY PLAINTIFF REBECCA BROWN.

THE PLAINTIFF IS DEEMED TO HAVE STIPULATED THAT THE MOTION WILL BE DECIDED BY THE PRO TEM JUDGE WITH THE SAME AUTHORITY AS A SUPERIOR COURT JUDGE.

THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT FAMILY SERVICE AGENCY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion To Deem Request For Admissions Admitted, Compel Responses To Discovery, And For Sanctions is GRANTED IN PART. Plaintiff has failed to respond to any of the discovery propounded by Defendant Family Service Agency of San Francisco that is the subject of Defendant's motion, and has thus waived all objections to the discovery. All of the requests for admission in Defendant Family Service Agency of San Francisco's Requests for Admission, Set One (consisting of Requests for Admission Nos. 1 through 22) are DEEMED ADMITTED. Plaintiff shall provide written responses, without objection, to Defendant's Form Interrogatories - General, Set One; Form Interrogatories - Employment Law, Set One; Special Interrogatories, Set One, and Requests for Production, Set One no later than June 29th. Defendant's requests for sanctions is GRANTED IN PART. Plaintiff shall pay Defendant the sum of $1,640 by June 29th.

SEE WRITTEN ORDER.

JUDGE PRO TEM ERNST HALPERIN, NOT REPORTED.
